codeReview.summary: Clean dead code removal. Worker methodically grepped every item before removal, correctly identified that RasterScratch and GlyphOutline (which the feature description claimed were used by the SVG rasterizer) actually had zero callers outside the dead code itself, and removed them too. The SVG rasterizer uses its own SvgRasterScratch/SvgSegment types, not the drawing library's RasterScratch/Segment. All retained items (OVERSAMPLE_X, OVERSAMPLE_Y, scale_fu, scale_fu_ceil) have verified live callers in lib.rs and svg.rs. The include!() references in lib.rs still point to both files. No #[allow(dead_code)] annotations remain. 539 lines removed across 2 files (rasterizer.rs: 489 lines removed, truetype.rs: 58 lines removed). Build succeeds with zero warnings, all 1462 tests pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature description in features.json incorrectly stated that RasterScratch and GlyphOutline were 'used by SVG rasterizer' and should be kept. The worker's grep disproved this — the SVG rasterizer uses its own SvgRasterScratch type. The worker correctly followed the 'grep to verify each one' instruction over the incorrect keep-list. Feature descriptions for dead-code-removal tasks should be grep-verified before writing, or the description should defer entirely to grep evidence rather than asserting specific items to keep.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature description says 'KEEP: RasterScratch and GlyphOutline (used by SVG rasterizer)'. Worker grep of svg.rs and lib.rs found zero references to drawing::RasterScratch or drawing::GlyphOutline. SVG rasterizer uses SvgRasterScratch (svg.rs line ~70) with SvgSegment, not drawing's types.

summary: Pass. The worker executed a thorough, evidence-driven dead code removal. Every item was grep-verified before removal. The worker correctly deviated from the feature description's keep-list when grep evidence showed RasterScratch and GlyphOutline had zero live callers, documenting the discrepancy in the handoff's discoveredIssues. The resulting files are minimal and clean: rasterizer.rs retains only the two oversampling constants with live callers, truetype.rs retains only the two scaling functions with live callers. Build and tests pass. No issues found.
